steps.title: Factor the numerator
steps.content: Both terms in the numerator share the factor $(x-7)$: $$8x(x-7)-3(x-7)=(x-7)(8x-3).$$

steps.title: Factor the denominator and cancel
steps.content: Factor $2$ from the denominator: $2x-14=2(x-7)$. Because $x>7$, the factor $(x-7)$ is nonzero and can be canceled: $$\frac{(x-7)(8x-3)}{2(x-7)}=\frac{8x-3}{2}.$$

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• A) $\frac{x-7}{5}$ — loses the $8x-3$ factor entirely. Eliminated.
  • B) $\frac{8x-3}{2}$ — matches the simplified expression.
  • C) $\frac{8x^{2}-3x-14}{2x-14}$ — equals $\frac{x(8x-3)-14}{2(x-7)}$, which does not equal the original. Eliminated.
  • D) $\frac{8x^{2}-3x-77}{2x-14}$ — $8x^{2}-3x-77$ is not divisible by $(x-7)$: plugging in $x=7$ gives $8(49)-3(7)-77=392-21-77=294\neq 0$, so it cannot simplify to $\frac{8x-3}{2}$. Eliminated.
That matches choice B — B is correct.
